script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'metrics_dashboard_redesign',
  description: 'Redesign MetricsTab.vue - compact 2-column layout, fix charts, add legend/tooltips, Keenetic style',
  phases: [{ title: 'Analyze' }, { title: 'Implement' }, { title: 'Verify' }]
};

phase('Analyze');

const analysis = agent(`Analyze the MetricsTab.vue dashboard at D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue

The user reported these problems (from screenshot review):
1. Charts are too big, don't fit at 100% zoom - need to be ~300x500px, use 2-column layout
2. Top 2 charts should be in 2 columns side-by-side with borders/frames
3. Bottom chart (outbound bar) - Y-axis labels overlap each other
4. Top charts (sparklines) - no tooltip values on hover
5. No legend on any chart
6. Charts are "smoothed" - should NOT be smoothed (remove tension)
7. Charts redraw every second - should animate/transition smoothly, not full redraw
8. General layout needs to be compact, lacmonic, beautiful in Keenetic dark style
9. Everything should fit without scrolling ideally

The current code uses:
- Chart.js with vue-chartjs (Line, Bar, Doughnut)
- Vue 3 composition API
- Scoped CSS with CSS variables from Keenetic dark theme

Current file is at: D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue

Read the file completely and report:
1. All specific issues to fix
2. The exact chart options that need changing
3. CSS layout changes needed for 2-column compact design
4. Animation options to prevent full redraw`, { label: 'analyze issues', model: 'openrouter/qwen/qwen3.6-27b' });

phase('Implement');

const implement = agent(`Fix the MetricsTab.vue dashboard at D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue

PROBLEMS TO FIX (all of them):

## 1. Layout - Make compact with 2-column grid
- Overview cards (Downlink/Uplink) should be in 2 columns, compact
- Stats cards (Outbound count, Health) in 2nd row, also compact  
- Outbound bar chart and Observatory section should be in 2 columns side by side
- Each section needs a border/frame (already has menu-background + menu-border, ensure visible)
- Everything should fit in viewport without scrolling if possible
- Use grid layout, cards ~300px wide

## 2. Charts - Fix rendering issues
- **Remove smoothing**: change tension from 0.4 to 0 (straight lines, no curves)  
- **Stop full redraw**: set animation.duration to 0 on sparkline data updates (use transitions instead)
- **Add tooltips on sparklines**: enable tooltip with formatBytes values, show on hover
- **Add legend**: show legend on outbound bar chart and sparklines with colored indicators
- **Fix Y-axis overlap on outbound bar**: reduce font size, add padding, truncate long labels with ellipsis
- **Compact chart heights**: sparklines ~50px, bar chart ~250px max, doughnut ~140px

## 3. Specific Chart.js option changes:

### Sparklines (sparkDownOptions, sparkUpOptions):
- tension: 0 (not 0.4)
- Enable tooltip with formatBytes callback
- animation.duration: 0 (prevent full redraw)
- Keep pointRadius: 0 but set pointHitRadius: 10 for tooltip hover area

### Outbound bar (outboundBarOptions):
- y-axis ticks: font size 10, add callback to truncate labels > 15 chars
- Add legend display
- animation.duration: 0 or very short

### Doughnut:
- Keep as is, already looks ok

## 4. CSS changes:
- .metrics-overview: grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr (always 2 cols, not 4)
- Add a new 2-column grid for bottom sections (outbound + observatory)
- .metrics-section__bar-chart height: reduce from 220px to fit in compact layout
- Cards should have consistent borders and spacing
- Make everything feel "Keenetic" - dark, minimal, precise

## 5. Chart data updates without redraw
The key issue is that computed properties recreate the data object every poll (3s), causing Chart.js to fully redraw. 
To fix: keep animation.duration = 0 and let Chart.js just update data points.
Actually the best approach: since data is in refs that change, vue-chartjs re-renders. We need to make updates smoother.
One approach: reduce poll interval visual impact by setting animation to 0 and using CSS transitions for the feel.

## IMPORTANT:
- Read the current file first with read tool
- Apply ALL changes using edit tool
- Do NOT rewrite the entire file - use targeted edits
- Keep all existing functionality
- Test that the file is valid Vue SFC after edits

The file is at: D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue`, { label: 'implement fixes' });

agents.label: analyze issues
agents.prompt:

3. Bottom chart (outbound bar) - Y-axis labels overlap each other
4. Top charts (sparklines) - no tooltip values on hover
5. No legend on any chart
6. Charts are "smoothed" - should NOT be smoothed (remove tension)
7. Charts redraw every second - should animate/transition smoothly, not full redraw
8. General layout needs to be compact, lacmonic, beautiful in Keenetic dark style
9. Everything should fit without scrolling ideally

The current code uses:
- Chart.js with vue-chartjs (Line, Bar, Doughnut)

- Scoped CSS with CSS variables from Keenetic dark theme

agents.label: implement fixes
agents.prompt:
Fix the MetricsTab.vue dashboard at D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue

## 2. Charts - Fix rendering issues

- **Add tooltips on sparklines**: enable tooltip with formatBytes values, show on hover

- Keep pointRadius: 0 but set pointHitRadius: 10 for tooltip hover area

### Outbound bar (outboundBarOptions):
- y-axis ticks: font size 10, add callback to truncate labels > 15 chars

## 4. CSS changes:

- Add a new 2-column grid for bottom sections (outbound + observatory)
- .metrics-section__bar-chart height: reduce from 220px to fit in compact layout

- Make everything feel "Keenetic" - dark, minimal, precise

## 5. Chart data updates without redraw
The key issue is that computed properties recreate the data object every poll (3s), causing Chart.js to fully redraw. 
To fix: keep animation.duration = 0 and let Chart.js just update data points.
Actually the best approach: since data is in refs that change, vue-chartjs re-renders. We need to make updates smoother.
One approach: reduce poll interval visual impact by setting animation to 0 and using CSS transitions for the feel.

## IMPORTANT:

- Apply ALL changes using edit tool
- Do NOT rewrite the entire file - use targeted edits
- Keep all existing functionality
- Test that the file is valid Vue SFC after edits

The file is at: D:/Documents/Repositories/xkeen-ui/xkeen-go/web/src/components/MetricsTab.vue
